message:
fix(hostcall_trace_jit): distinguish guard failure from trace invalidation in deopt reporting and stop counting deopts after invalidation

Changes the JIT trace compiler's guard failure handling to provide more
precise deopt diagnostics and prevent inflated telemetry after a trace
has already been invalidated:

Logic changes in record_guard_failure:
- Returns Option<u64> instead of unit: None when the plan is still live
  (normal guard miss), Some(total_failures) when this failure crossed the
  threshold and invalidated the trace
- Adds an `!profile.invalidated` guard so that a plan that has already been
  invalidated does not increment the invalidation counter again, which
  previously caused silent over-counting in telemetry.deopts and
  telemetry.invalidations

Logic changes in try_jit_dispatch:
- Uses the new return value to select between DeoptReason::GuardFailure
  (still-live trace, includes guard_index and description) and
  DeoptReason::TraceInvalidated (threshold just crossed, includes
  total_failures count), giving callers an unambiguous signal about
  whether the trace was evicted on this call

New tests:
- jit_dispatch_deopt_on_support_count_guard: verifies GuardFailure with
  guard_index=2 when MinSupportCount guard trips
- threshold_crossing_failure_reports_trace_invalidated: verifies the
  exact transition from GuardFailure → TraceInvalidated → NotCompiled
  across consecutive misses, and checks invalidation/deopt/miss counters
- deopts_stop_growing_after_invalidation (proptest): verifies that for
  arbitrary max_guard_failures (1..8) and attempts (1..40), telemetry.deopts
  is capped at min(attempts, max_guard_failures) and invalidations ≤ 1

Also includes minor rustfmt reformatting of the arb_config proptest strategy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
